page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A defining hardware characteristic is the inclusion of 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, all managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7ie.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU and memory performance. It achieved top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 93,157.6. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 548.31 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems, while static web serving throughput measured 128.24 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.24xlarge offers high resource density by bundling 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage with 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory. This configuration minimizes the need for external network-attached storage for data-heavy operations, optimizing the resource-to-cost ratio for storage-intensive workloads. The server is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs running at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, complemented by a 50 Gbps baseline network speed. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier performance, notably in Passmark CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core metrics, and multi-threaded decompression. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving and database operations. Given its massive local storage capacity and high memory allocation, the i7ie.24xlarge is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput transactional workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage designed for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six Intel vCPUs and sixty terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
60,000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier CPU performance in Passmark and Geekbench benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases and data warehousing
